name: flowplugin
description: Download, edit, and re-upload Power Automate cloud flows as unpacked solutions, powered by the Microsoft Power Platform CLI (pac).

untrustedWorkspaces.description: In untrusted workspaces, workspace-scoped 'flowplugin.pacPath' and 'flowplugin.solutionsRoot' settings are ignored; user/global values are used instead.

commands.title: Power Automate: Compare Flow with Server
Power Automate: Pull Flow and Discard Local Changes
Power Automate: Install Flow Skill into Workspace

languageModelTools.name: flowplugin_downloadSolution
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Download (pull) a Power Platform solution from the selected environment and unpack its flows into the workspace. Use when the user asks to download, pull, refresh, or sync down a solution. The `solutionName` argument is OPTIONAL: if the user does not specify a solution by name, omit the argument and the tool will use the solution already pinned to this workspace. Examples: 'download solution', 'pull the solution', 'download the contoso_core solution', 'refresh my solution from the environment'. Do NOT use this tool to upload or push local changes back to the server — that is the uploadFlow tool.
languageModelTools.userDescription: Download a Power Platform solution and unpack its flows into the workspace.

solutionName.description: OPTIONAL. Unique name (preferred) or friendly name of the Power Platform solution to download. Omit to use the solution already pinned to this workspace.

languageModelTools.name: flowplugin_uploadFlow
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Upload (sync) a single Power Automate flow from the workspace back to Dataverse. Use when the user asks to upload, sync, push, or publish a flow. The tool detects server-side drift; if the server version changed since the last download, the user is asked whether to overwrite, pull and discard local changes, or view the diff first. Examples: 'upload my flow', 'sync the Approval flow', 'push the Notification flow'. The `solutionName` argument is OPTIONAL: omit it to use the solution pinned to this workspace. The `flowName` argument is also OPTIONAL when the solution contains exactly one flow — omit it to upload that single flow without prompting. Do NOT use this tool to download or pull a solution — that is the downloadSolution tool.
languageModelTools.userDescription: Upload a Power Automate flow from the workspace back to the selected environment.

solutionName.description: OPTIONAL. Unique name (preferred) or friendly name of the solution that contains the flow. Omit to use the solution pinned to this workspace.

flowName.description: OPTIONAL. Display name of the flow to upload, or its workflow GUID. Omit when the solution contains exactly one flow — the tool will upload that single flow automatically.

flowplugin.pacPath.description: Path to the Microsoft Power Platform CLI executable.

flowplugin.solutionsRoot.description: Workspace-relative folder where downloaded solutions are unpacked.

flowplugin.autoPublishOnUpload.description: Publish the flow immediately after a successful upload.

flowplugin.autoPublishOnImport.description: Deprecated. Use 'flowplugin.autoPublishOnUpload'. Read for backward compatibility only.

flowplugin.packageType.description: Package type used by pac solution export/pack.

flowplugin.lint.blockOnWarnings.description: Treat lint warnings as errors and block uploads when any are present.

flowplugin.checkConnectionsBeforeUpload.description: Before uploading a flow, query Dataverse to confirm each connection reference it uses is bound to an active connection in the target environment.

flowplugin.driftDetection.description: Capture per-flow metadata (modifiedon, ETag) at download time and compare against the live server state before upload. Prompts on remote drift.

flowplugin.deactivateBeforeUpload.description: Before patching the flow definition, deactivate the flow if it is currently Activated, then reactivate after the update succeeds. Avoids unintended trigger executions during the update window.

flowplugin.backupRetention.description: Number of timestamped pre-upload backups to keep per flow under '.flowplugin/backups/'. 0 keeps everything.

flowplugin.dryRunUpload.description: When enabled, validate and back up the remote clientdata but do not send the PATCH. Useful for testing the safe-upload pipeline.
